codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the requested behavior: process_exec payloads now include numeric ppid, readPPID parses /proc/<pid>/stat robustly across comm edge cases using the closing ')' boundary, and tests cover required success/error scenarios plus payload round-trip checks without regressions to existing fields.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The notify-worker skill procedure is over-broad for ebpf-only features: it directs workers to run internal/af-focused validations even when the feature touches only internal/ebpf, which causes unrelated failures/workarounds and noisy execution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 0f5e61a2-c512-40a0-a28e-1a0127efd88d shows early attempts to run go test ./internal/af and go mod tidy before ebpf work; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges also calls out this mismatch explicitly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/services.yaml does not include ebpf-scoped test/vet commands even though this mission's validation contract requires them for ppid enrichment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-EVT-002/003/004 references go test ./internal/ebpf and go vet ./internal/ebpf, while .factory/services.yaml only defines test_go for ./internal/af ./internal/classifier and lint_go for ./internal/....

summary: Review outcome: pass. I found no blocking correctness or security defects in the ppid-enrichment implementation; behavior and tests align with the feature contract.
